title: Quararibea stenopetala
taxonomy: Quararibea stenopetala belongs to the family Ochnaceae and is classified in the order Malpighiales. It is a species of flowering plant native to Central and South America. The family Ochnaceae consists of about 30 genera and over 500 species of plants. Quararibea stenopetala is one of the many species within the Quararibea genus. This plant species is considered to be part of the larger group known as flowering plants, or angiosperms.
uses: Quararibea stenopetala is used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Amazon region, where its bark is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is also reported to have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. Its leaves and bark are sometimes used to prepare infusions for medicinal purposes. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Quararibea stenopetala as a medicinal plant. The plant's medicinal uses are not widely recognized outside of traditional Amazonian medicine.

The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which can aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues.
